name: v4
context: Tailwind CSS v4 — CSS-first config, @theme, Oxide engine, new features, breaking changes, and v3 migration
info: Tailwind CSS v4 (released January 2025) is a ground-up rewrite that moves configuration from JavaScript to CSS, dramatically improves build performance (up to 182x faster incremental builds), and adds major features: built-in container queries, 3D transforms, expanded gradient APIs, @starting-style support, and a modernized OKLCH color palette. The most significant change for agents is that tailwind.config.js is replaced by @theme in CSS, the three @tailwind directives become a single @import "tailwindcss", and content detection is automatic. This node teaches agents to recognize v3 vs v4 projects, understand the new directives (@theme, @utility, @custom-variant, @plugin, @source), and migrate between versions.
triggers: what is new in tailwind v4
is this tailwind v3 or v4

sections.title: Identifying v3 vs v4 projects
sections.body: v3 indicators: `tailwind.config.js` or `tailwind.config.ts` exists, CSS uses `@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;`, `content` array in config, PostCSS uses `tailwindcss` plugin. v4 indicators: no config file, CSS uses `@import "tailwindcss"`, has `@theme` blocks, PostCSS uses `@tailwindcss/postcss`, may have `@tailwindcss/vite` in vite.config.

sections.title: CSS-first configuration with @theme
sections.body: The `@theme` directive in CSS replaces `tailwind.config.js`. Define `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--breakpoint-*`, `--spacing`, `--radius-*`, `--shadow-*`, `--ease-*`, `--animate-*` variables. They compile to CSS custom properties AND generate utility classes. Use `--namespace-*: initial;` to remove defaults before adding custom values. Theme variables are accessible via `var()` in any CSS.

sections.title: New v4 directives
sections.body: `@import "tailwindcss"` replaces three @tailwind directives. `@theme {}` defines design tokens. `@utility name {}` creates custom utilities with variant support. `@custom-variant name (selector)` adds custom variants. `@source "path"` adds content detection paths. `@plugin "package"` loads JS plugins. `@reference "tailwindcss"` imports types without CSS output. `@config "path"` loads v3 config for compatibility.

sections.title: New features in v4
sections.body: Built-in container queries (@container, @sm:, @md:). Max-width breakpoint variants (max-md:). 3D transforms (rotate-x-*, perspective-*). Expanded gradients (bg-linear-45, bg-conic, bg-radial, color interpolation with /oklch). OKLCH color palette. Cascade layers. not-* variant for negation. @starting-style for entry animations. field-sizing for auto-resize textareas. inset-shadow-* and inset-ring-* for layered shadows.

sections.title: v3 to v4 migration guide
sections.body: Run `npx @tailwindcss/upgrade` for automated migration. Manual steps: (1) install tailwindcss@latest and @tailwindcss/postcss, (2) update postcss.config.js, (3) replace @tailwind directives with @import "tailwindcss", (4) move theme.extend to @theme CSS block, (5) move plugins to @plugin directives, (6) remove content config, (7) rename bg-gradient-* to bg-linear-*, (8) delete tailwind.config.js. Use @config for incremental migration.

sections.title: Performance improvements
sections.body: v4 uses the Oxide engine (Rust-based). Full builds are 3.78x faster (378ms → 100ms). Incremental rebuilds with new CSS are 8.8x faster (44ms → 5ms). Incremental rebuilds with no new CSS are 182x faster (35ms → 192 microseconds). Built-in @import support eliminates postcss-import. Automatic content detection eliminates file-list overhead.

v4 uses @custom-variant instead of darkMode config
